_thread: Thread C — AWS RDS credential rotation runbook
_notes: Best thread for how-to extraction. Schema fits very well. The procedural nature maps directly to steps_taken.
title: Rotating AWS RDS Credentials Without Downtime (Secrets Manager)
summary: Quarterly process for rotating RDS credentials using AWS Secrets Manager. The rotation Lambda type must match your rotation strategy — single-user vs multi-user (our environment uses SecretsManagerRDSMySQLRotationMultiUser). After rotation, a rolling restart of the API fleet is required for apps to pick up the new secret. Total time is ~30 minutes including buffer. The window of potential DB auth errors is ~5–8 minutes between rotation trigger and rolling restart completion.
steps_taken: Go to AWS Secrets Manager → select secret /prod/rds/api-db → choose 'Rotate secret immediately'
Confirm rotation Lambda is configured — use SecretsManagerRDSMySQLRotationMultiUser for multi-user rotation
Trigger rotation and watch CloudWatch logs for the Lambda execution
Perform rolling restart of the API fleet to pick up the new secret
Verify app logs for DB auth errors after restart — allow 5–8 min window
resolution: Credentials rotated successfully. No DB auth errors post-restart. Apps reconnected cleanly.
root_cause: N/A — scheduled compliance process, not triggered by an incident.
